div.head: The Consul General at Tientsin (Caldwell) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
39. My 38, March 20, 2 p.mNot printed. Lieutenant Colonel JamesCommanding U. S. Marine Guard at Tientsin. this afternoon discussed with a Japanese military staff officer the matter
of protection of Americans residing in the first special area and was assured by him that their safety was receiving the solicitous consideration of the Japanese military who are particularly desirous of avoiding injury to Americans; and that police and gendarmes in the first special area have been increased since the kidnapping of Dyott, Chairman of British Chamber of Commerce.The Consul General at Tientsin reported the release of Dyott on April 3 (393.4111/34).
Repeated to Peiping, Chungking, Shanghai. By mail to Tokyo.
